name: Hardik Shrestha
summary: Associate program manager candidate with experience in reporting, SOPs, quality documentation, recurring operations, and cross-functional coordination across finance, data, and platform teams.

entries.title: Honours Bachelor of Science, Computer Science | Honours Business Administration (HBA)
bullets: Dean's Honour List | 3.9/4.0 cGPA | Wellington Financial LP Award

sections.title: Core Skills
sections.text: Program operations, reporting, process improvement, SOPs and work instructions, QA documentation, stakeholder coordination, change management, quality improvement, Excel, VBA, SQL, Python, Power BI, Power Automate, French

entries.title: Finance Analyst Intern, Marketing & Media Finance
bullets: Automated media accruals, vendor accruals, and recurring Excel/VBA reporting workflows, saving 26 hours per reporting period while improving reliability for finance deliverables.
Owned recurring reporting and large-data-set reconciliation for internal stakeholders, supporting monthly finance execution.
Co-developed the winning LoblawTank proposal for a Flashfood x Too Good To Go integration, building the business case and presenting recommendations to senior strategy and sustainability leaders.

entries.title: Data Analyst, Swim Guide
bullets: Designed and maintained automated ETL and data-validation workflows across multi-source beach data, saving 20+ staff hours per week while improving operational reliability.
Led ID reconciliation and GPS mapping across 3,000+ managed beaches during a backend overhaul, improving data integrity.
Validated fixes and improved documentation during migration from manual checks to Power Automate, PostgreSQL, and Java-based workflows.

entries.title: Quality Assurance Intern, AI Platform
bullets: Built automated validation workflows for AI-platform releases using Docker, Kubernetes, Java, and Git, reducing turnaround time by 80% and strengthening release-readiness discipline.
Wrote engineering tickets and supported larger-scale API testing during fast-moving release cycles.
Produced QA documentation, SOPs, work instructions, and training material for GCS/AWS migration support, improving handoffs, repeatability, and execution consistency across cloud-transition work.

entries.title: Software Engineer Co-Op, Identity Access Management
bullets: Participated in backlog prioritization, wrote engineering tickets, and ran morning standups in an agile IAM team.
Built identity and access management features and bulk migration tooling using Azure, Java, React, Vue, JavaScript, and Postman, increasing user-migration throughput by 100x.
Supported rollout readiness through work instructions, training materials, and QA documentation for reusable workflows.

entries.title: Robotic Process Automation Intern, Operations
bullets: Built Power Automate, Selenium, and Zapier automations for client-facing workflows after gathering requirements with leadership, helping deliver secure systems on time for direct client use.
Redesigned post-training workflows and documentation, cutting manual follow-up work for all consultants by 90%.

entries.title: Independent Operations Finance Project
bullets: Built an Excel, SQL, and Python simulator translating operating inputs into weekly P/L views and variance dashboards.

entries.title: Research Project
bullets: Authored a 30-page report synthesizing 80+ sources on Canadian corporate climate adaptation, with findings used by UN GCNC.

entries.title: President
bullets: Restructured governance and launched digital systems, driving 260% year-over-year membership growth.
